Records.description: Two-loop non-planar crossed three-point function $\tilde{C}^{(2)}(p_1^2,p_2^2,p_3^2)$ with massless internal lines and three off-shell external momenta. Called $\tilde{C}^{(2)}$ in the paper. Factorizes in coordinate space as $\tilde{C}^{(2)} = (C^{(1)})^2 = (i\pi^2/p_3^2)^2 \cdot (\Phi^{(1)}(x,y))^2$. Weight 4.
Records.familyName: two-loop massless non-planar crossed triangle with off-shell external legs

Records.description: Two-loop HQET integral family for the three-Wilson-line three-gluon-vertex web contributing to the tripole soft anomalous dimension. 6 denominators (3 gluon + 3 eikonal, with IR regulator $\delta=1$ on $v_3$) plus 3 ISPs. Kira yields 16 masters with 4 in the top sector. Canonical DE via CANONICA/DlogBasis/INITIAL; UT basis through weight 4. 13-letter alphabet. Single pole $w^{(2,-1)}$ reproduces the known two-loop tripole anomalous dimension. Called `[3gv]` in the paper.
Records.familyName: 2-loop 3-point HQET web with 3-gluon vertex (one-leg IR regulator)

Records.description: Two-loop massless on-shell vertex (form-factor) family with internal loop momenta $l, r$ and external momenta $p, q$ satisfying $p^2 = q^2 = 0$ and $2 p \cdot q = -1$ (i.e. $(p+q)^2 = -1$, fixed normalization). Used in the FIRE6 manual to illustrate the LiteRed-rules workflow: CreateNewBasis[v2], GenerateIBP, AnalyzeSectors[v2,{0,__}] (the first listed entry $(l-r)^2$ is treated as an ISP via non-positive first index), FindSymmetries with EMs->True, and DiskSave. Only IBP reduction information (sectors, symmetries, boundary conditions) is generated; no master integrals are evaluated in the paper.
Records.location: Section 4.5 (Using LiteRed rules in Mathematica), code block around lines 604-637; Fig. 'vertex2' (Fig. 1, line 598)

Records.description: Two-loop massless non-planar form factor (crossed ladder) family with three external legs $q_1+q_2+q_3=0$ and $q_3^2=0$. An auxiliary scale $q_2^2 \neq 0$ is introduced so that the single-scale on-shell integral ($q_2^2=q_3^2=0$) is reached as a boundary limit; integrals depend on $x=q_2^2/q_1^2$. Seven UT basis integrals $f_1..f_7$ are constructed (three bubble products, three triangles, and the crossed-ladder $f_7$), satisfying a canonical DE in $x$ with alphabet $\{x,1-x\}$ (Fuchsian singularities at $x=0,1,\infty$). Boundary conditions follow from regularity of $f_4..f_7$ at $x=1$. Solution given as harmonic polylogarithms through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^2)$ in Eq. (6.16); the on-shell crossed ladder $f_7(0,\varepsilon)$ is computed through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^6)$ (Eq. (6.18)) in agreement with literature, the boundary at $x \to 0$ being extracted via the Drinfeld-associator/matching procedure of Section 6.3.
Records.familyName: two-loop non-planar massless form factor (crossed ladder) family

Records.description: Two-loop nonplanar vertex diagram with massless external legs, used as FIRE 6.5 benchmark. Kinematics fixed numerically (no free variables besides $d$). Reduction tested with rank-1 and rank-20 ISP numerators using LiteRed rules.

Records.authors: Bern, Zvi, Enciso, Michael, Ita, Harald, Zeng, Mao
Records.description: Two-loop nonplanar crossed triangle-box with 6 propagators and one ISP. Nonplanar dual conformal symmetry constructed. No integral evaluation.

Massless non-planar crossed double-box for the electroweak form factor f fbar -> X. All six propagators massless. This is a genuine master integral (cannot be reduced to simpler topologies by IBP). Computed in full from O(eps^{-4}) through O(eps^0) in terms of weight-4 HPLs of x=-s/m^2. The result is taken from Gonsalves (1983) and cross-checked against Van Neerven (1986); together with the planar ladder it is the only six-denominator amplitude with a fourth-order infrared pole.
Records.location: Section 5.2 (crossed ladders), Eq. with coefficients Q_{-4}..Q_0 (around line 4661--4694); denominator set {D_1, D_2, D_3, D_4, D_6, D_{11}} from appendix A1.

Records.authors: Bargiela, Piotr, Frellesvig, Hjalte, Marzucca, Robin, Morales, Roger, Seefeld, Florian, Wilhelm, Matthias, Yang, Tong-Zhi
Records.description: $I^{**}_{2,2,2}$ two-loop topology with three internal paths of lengths a=2, b=2, c=2 (neither v_L nor v_R carries external legs); 3-point integral, non-planar (b=>=2). Geometry: 1 ISP; max cut ∫dz/√P_4(z); elliptic curve (§3.4 Type 1.2)

Names: two-loop massless non-planar crossed triangle with off-shell external legs
2-loop 3-point HQET web with 3-gluon vertex (one-leg IR regulator)
two-loop non-planar massless form factor (crossed ladder) family
